name: Classify workflow errors with Claude and deduplicate Jira and Slack alerts

parameters.content:
## AI Error Observability — Claude Classification + Jira Deduplication

### How it works

This workflow observes n8n execution errors, normalizes the error payload, and asks Claude to classify the incident. It generates a deduplication hash, searches Jira for a matching issue, then either comments on the existing ticket or creates a new incident. After the Jira action completes, it sends a Slack alert summarizing what happened.

### Setup steps

- Configure the workflow as an n8n error workflow so the Error Trigger receives failed execution data.
- Add Anthropic credentials and select the intended Claude model for the classification chain.
- Configure Jira credentials, project/issue settings, and the search query or custom field/label used to match the generated error hash.
- Configure Slack credentials and choose the destination channel for alerts.
- Verify that Jira and Slack credentials have permission to search issues, create issues, add comments, and post messages.

### Customization

Adjust the Claude classification prompt, hash-generation logic, Jira project/fields, duplicate search criteria, ticket summary/description templates, and Slack message formatting to match your incident-management conventions.

## Capture and normalize

Receives workflow errors through the error trigger and reshapes the raw error data into a consistent payload for downstream AI analysis.

## Classify and fingerprint

Uses Claude via the Anthropic chat model to classify the normalized error, then parses the model output and generates a deterministic hash for deduplication.

## Check Jira duplicate

Searches Jira for an existing incident matching the generated error hash, prepares duplicate-check routing data, and branches based on whether a ticket already exists.

## Update existing ticket

Handles the upper duplicate branch by appending a recurrence comment to the existing Jira issue and standardizing the resulting Jira key and action fields.

## Create incident ticket

Handles the lower new-incident branch by creating a Jira ticket and standardizing the new issue key and action fields for later notification.

## Merge and alert

Converges the existing-ticket and new-ticket paths, builds a Slack-ready notification message, and sends the final alert to Slack.

nodes.name: When Error Detected
nodes.notes: Global error handler. Assign this workflow as the Error Workflow in Settings → Error Workflow for every production pipeline. Fires on both execution-level and trigger-level failures.

parameters.text:
=You are an expert Site Reliability Engineer specializing in n8n workflow automation. Analyze the following workflow execution error and return a structured JSON classification.

## Error Context
- Workflow: {{ $json.workflowName }} (ID: {{ $json.workflowId }})
- Execution ID: {{ $json.executionId }}
- Failing Node: {{ $json.failingNode }} (Type: {{ $json.failingNodeType }})
- Error Mode: {{ $json.errorMode }}
- Timestamp: {{ $json.timestamp }}

## Error Message
```
{{ $json.errorMessage }}
```

## Stack Trace{{ $json.stackTruncated ? ' [TRUNCATED AT 4000 chars]' : '' }}
```
{{ $json.errorStack }}
```

## Raw Payload Excerpt
```json
{{ $json.rawPayloadSnippet }}
```

---

Return ONLY a valid JSON object with this exact schema — no preamble, no markdown fences:
{
  "category": "<one of: RATE_LIMIT | AUTH_FAILURE | SCHEMA_DRIFT | NETWORK_TIMEOUT | CONFIG_ERROR | EMPTY_RESPONSE | PERMISSION_DENIED | UNKNOWN>",
  "severity": "<one of: CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW>",
  "summary": "<one concise sentence, max 120 chars, describing the root cause>",
  "root_cause": "<2-4 sentences of technical root cause analysis>",
  "recommended_action": "<2-3 sentences of concrete remediation steps>",
  "is_transient": <true if likely self-resolving (rate limits, transient timeouts), false if requires manual fix>
}

Classification rules:
- RATE_LIMIT: HTTP 429, quota exceeded, too many requests
- AUTH_FAILURE: HTTP 401/403, invalid token, expired credentials, API key issues
- SCHEMA_DRIFT: unexpected field, cannot read property of undefined, JSON parse errors from upstream
- NETWORK_TIMEOUT: ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT, ENOTFOUND, socket hang up
- CONFIG_ERROR: missing required parameter, invalid node configuration, expression evaluation failure
- EMPTY_RESPONSE: upstream returned 200 but empty body or empty array
- PERMISSION_DENIED: insufficient scope, ACL rejection, OAuth scope missing
- UNKNOWN: none of the above

Severity rules:
- CRITICAL: data loss risk, payment/compliance workflows, cascading failure
- HIGH: production pipeline blocked, customer-facing impact
- MEDIUM: non-critical automation degraded, retryable
- LOW: dev/test workflows, informational

nodes.name: Claude Model
nodes.notes: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001: structured extraction from a fixed schema — Haiku handles it at a fraction of Opus cost and latency. 15s timeout prevents LLM hangs from blocking the pipeline during cascading failures.

nodes.name: Search Jira Ticket
nodes.notes: JQL: finds any open (statusCategory != Done) issue with the dedupHash label. Tickets closed as Won't Fix or Duplicate are statusCategory = Done — a new error of the same type will create a fresh ticket. This is intentional. Set JIRA_PROJECT_KEY as an n8n env var.

options.jql: =project = "{{ $env.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}" AND labels = "n8n-dedup-{{ $json.dedupHash }}" AND statusCategory != Done ORDER BY created DESC

nodes.name: If Ticket Exists
nodes.notes: Output 0 (TRUE) → open ticket found → append recurrence comment → Set Jira Key (comment). Output 1 (FALSE) → no open ticket → create new Bug → Set Jira Key (create). Both paths converge at Merge before Build Slack.

nodes.name: Append Comment to Jira Ticket
nodes.notes: operation: 'add' is the correct value per IssueCommentDescription.ts — 'create' does not exist on this resource. Parameter is 'issueKey' and body field is 'comment' (not 'commentBody'). wikiMarkup: true forces API v2 so Atlassian Wiki Markup in the comment body renders correctly on Jira Cloud.

nodes.name: Create Jira Incident Ticket
nodes.notes: Creates a new Jira Bug. Labels: n8n-error, n8n-dedup-{hash} (JQL anchor), error category, transient flag, and llm-fallback when Claude was unavailable (JQL: project = X AND labels = 'llm-fallback' to audit LLM availability).

nodes.name: Prepare Comment Branch Data
nodes.notes: Stamps jiraKey and action onto the item from the comment branch. Merge node downstream reads $json.jiraKey regardless of which branch ran.

nodes.name: Prepare Create Branch Data
nodes.notes: Stamps jiraKey ($json.key from Jira create response) and action onto the item from the create branch. Merge node downstream reads $json.jiraKey regardless of which branch ran.

nodes.name: Merge Data from Branches
nodes.notes: chooseBranch mode: forwards whichever input arrives without waiting for the other. Since only one branch (comment or create) executes per run, this is the correct mode — append and combine both wait for all inputs. Build Slack Notification reads $json.jiraKey which is always stamped by the Set nodes upstream.

nodes.name: Send Alert to Slack
nodes.notes: Slack Bot Token credential with chat:write scope. Set SLACK_CHANNEL_ID as an n8n env var.
